title: Morphology of the Folktale
author: Vladimir Propp
source: Morphology of the Folktale (1928); Russian Formalist analysis of ~100 Russian fairy tales.

lineage.note: Russian Formalist; the scientific, bottom-up counterpart to Campbell's mystical, top-down monomyth. Direct ancestor of computational/structuralist story analysis. Propp extracted 31 narrative functions and 7 character roles. Stable: the SEQUENCE of functions, not the surface content.

stages.name: Absentation
stages.function: A member of the family leaves home.

stages.name: Interdiction
stages.function: A prohibition is addressed to the hero.

stages.name: Violation
stages.function: The prohibition is violated.

stages.name: Reconnaissance
stages.function: The villain seeks information.

stages.name: Delivery
stages.function: The villain receives information about the victim.

stages.name: Trickery
stages.function: Villain attempts to deceive.

stages.name: Complicity
stages.function: Victim falls for trick or unknowingly helps.

stages.name: Villainy or Lack
stages.function: Villain causes harm OR a family member lacks something. (This is the inciting incident.)

stages.name: Mediation
stages.function: Misfortune made known; hero is dispatched.

stages.name: Beginning Counteraction
stages.function: Hero decides to act.

stages.name: Departure
stages.function: Hero leaves home.

stages.name: First Function of the Donor
stages.function: Hero is tested, attacked, or interrogated; prepares to receive a magical agent.

stages.name: Hero's Reaction
stages.function: Hero reacts to the donor's test.

stages.name: Receipt of Magical Agent
stages.function: Hero acquires the gift, helper, or knowledge.

stages.name: Guidance
stages.function: Hero is led to the object of search.

stages.name: Struggle
stages.function: Hero and villain join in direct combat.

stages.name: Branding
stages.function: Hero is marked (wound, ring, scar).

stages.name: Victory
stages.function: Villain is defeated.

stages.name: Liquidation
stages.function: Initial misfortune or lack is resolved.

stages.name: Return
stages.function: Hero returns.

stages.name: Pursuit
stages.function: Hero is chased.

stages.name: Rescue
stages.function: Hero is rescued from pursuit.

stages.name: Unrecognized Arrival
stages.function: Hero arrives home or elsewhere, unrecognized.

stages.name: Unfounded Claims
stages.function: A false hero presents claims.

stages.name: Difficult Task
stages.function: A difficult task is proposed to the hero.

stages.name: Solution
stages.function: The task is resolved.

stages.name: Recognition
stages.function: Hero is recognized by the mark.

stages.name: Exposure
stages.function: False hero or villain is exposed.

stages.name: Transfiguration
stages.function: Hero is given a new appearance.

stages.name: Punishment
stages.function: Villain is punished.

stages.name: Wedding
stages.function: Hero marries and ascends the throne (or equivalent reward).

applicability.limitations: Propp generalized from Russian fairy tales specifically; the 31-function sequence doesn't survive intact in stories outside that genre. Most useful as a checklist of FUNCTION TYPES rather than a strict sequence.
